Title: Differentially evolved glucosyltransferases determine natural variation of rice flavone accumulation and UV-tolerance

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) for overexpression in rice ZH11
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ZH11 for transgenic assays; natural variation analyzed across 529 rice accessions; Nipponbare used for cloning/recombinant protein
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong positive allele can be introduced into elite rice cultivars to elevate flavone 7-O-glycosides and improve UV-B tolerance. Best deployment is likely as allele mining/editing or transgenic overexpression, potentially stacked with OsUGT707A2 because combined high-flavone-glucoside genotypes were enriched in high-UV regions. Field validation is still needed because greenhouse UV assays may not fully predict field performance.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to the 7-hydroxyl group of flavones, forming flavone 7-O-glucosides; also shows activity on some flavonols in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of flavone 7-O-glucosides (including apigenin 7-O-glucoside and flavone 7-O-rutinoside-related increases in planta) and enhances UV-B tolerance when overexpressed.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS association mapping; recombinant protein enzyme assays and kinetics; LC-MS/HPLC product identification with authentic standards; transgenic overexpression in rice; metabolite profiling; UV-B survival assays; NBT staining
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms major decorated end products (flavone 7-O-glucosides)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UV-B irradiation; comparison of field sites with different UV-B irradiance (Hainan versus Wuhan); geographic UV-B gradient across rice origins
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in rice; higher expression than other duplicated UGTs examined, but transcript level was not significantly correlated with apigenin 7-O-glucoside variation among varieties
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in rice; higher than related duplicated UGTs in the study
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics (widely targeted LC-MS/MS flavonoid profiling); mGWAS using previous rice metabolite and SNP datasets from 529 accessions; qRT-PCR for expression analysis; no public accession reported in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Allelic variation mainly acts through protein catalytic efficiency rather than transcript abundance. Two alleles differed by about 100-fold in kcat/Km. Multiple nonsynonymous SNPs, including one near the putative acceptor-binding pocket, were associated with natural variation. In vitro it can also glycosylate flavonols, but in planta overexpression primarily increased flavones.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious pre-UV growth penalty reported. OsUGT706D1 overexpression improved UV-B tolerance but was less effective than OsUGT707A2. Grain-weight benefit under natural high-UV field conditions was specifically shown for OsUGT707A2, not established for OsUGT706D1.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Flavones such as apigenin and tricin; UDP-glucose as sugar donor
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase (UGT); PSPG box-containing glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal tailoring step controlling natural variation of major rice flavones
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Nipponbare for cloned coding sequence; allelic diversity assessed across diverse accessions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsUGT706D1 encodes the major rice flavone 7-O-glucosyltransferase that uses UDP-glucose to produce flavone 7-O-glucosides. 2. Natural variation in apigenin 7-O-glucoside is mainly explained by coding polymorphisms that alter enzyme activity rather than transcript abundance. 3. Overexpression increases flavone 7-O-glycosides and improves UV-B tolerance, supporting use of strong alleles for stress-tolerant rice improvement.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: UV-B protection; antioxidant/ROS attenuation; potential contribution to crop stress tolerance and dietary phytochemical value

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) for overexpression in rice ZH11
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ZH11 for transgenic assays; natural variation analyzed across 529 rice accessions; Nipponbare used for cloning/recombinant protein
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A favorable allele or engineered high-expression allele is a promising target for breeding or editing to raise flavone 5-O-glucosides and improve UV-B tolerance, especially in high-irradiance environments. It is an attractive stacking partner with OsUGT706D1, but real field gain still requires validation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to the 5-hydroxyl group of flavones, forming flavone 5-O-glucosides such as apigenin 5-O-glucoside and tricin 5-O-glucoside; also shows some activity on flavonols in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of flavone 5-O-glucosides and confers strong UV-B tolerance; overexpression lines showed higher survival and higher hundred-grain weight than WT under a higher-UV field environment.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS association mapping; recombinant protein enzyme assays and kinetics; LC-MS/HPLC product identification with authentic standards and MS2; transgenic overexpression in rice; metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR; UV-B survival assays; NBT staining; field grain-weight comparison under contrasting UV environments
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms major decorated end products (flavone 5-O-glucosides)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UV-B irradiation; field comparison under different natural UV-B irradiance (Hainan versus Wuhan); geographic UV-B gradient across accession origins
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in rice and more highly expressed than other duplicated UGTs; transcript level positively correlated with apigenin 5-O-glucoside content across tested varieties
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in rice; relatively high among duplicated UGTs studied
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics (widely targeted LC-MS/MS flavonoid profiling); mGWAS using previous rice metabolite and SNP datasets from 529 accessions; qRT-PCR for expression analysis; no public accession reported in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Natural variation is mainly linked to expression polymorphism and a rare loss-of-function coding InDel. SNP sf0719059405 located ~400 bp upstream was strongly associated with apigenin 5-O-glucoside content and expression; InDel vf0719060607 caused a frameshift truncating the UDP-glucose-binding region in very low-producer varieties. Apigenin 5-O-glucoside showed distinctive bright yellow fluorescence under UV-B, and OsUGT707A2 overexpression gave stronger UV-B protection than OsUGT706D1.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth penalty before UV treatment. Benefit to hundred-grain weight was observed in high-UV Hainan but not low-UV Wuhan, indicating context dependence. Field translation beyond tested environments remains uncertain.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Flavones such as apigenin and tricin; UDP-glucose as sugar donor
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase (UGT); PSPG box-containing glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal tailoring step controlling natural variation of major rice flavones
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Nipponbare for cloned coding sequence; allelic diversity assessed across diverse accessions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsUGT707A2 encodes the major rice flavone 5-O-glucosyltransferase producing apigenin 5-O-glucoside and related flavone 5-O-glucosides from UDP-glucose. 2. Natural variation is largely explained by promoter-associated expression polymorphism and a rare frameshift loss-of-function allele. 3. Overexpression strongly increases flavone 5-O-glucosides, enhances UV-B tolerance, lowers ROS accumulation, and can improve grain weight under high-UV field conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: UV-B protection; antioxidant/ROS attenuation; potential contribution to crop stress tolerance and dietary phytochemical value
